landing.desc: {{count}} dimensions, scenario-based questions to assess decision-making compatibility.
landing.subdesc: Each person answers independently. Results show a radar comparison. No right answers — only alignment.

features.tiers: Three depth levels to choose from

transition.title: Pass the phone to Person B
transition.desc: Person A has finished. Please answer independently without discussing.

results.alignedDesc: {{dims}} — very close alignment, a stable foundation.
results.moderateDesc: {{dims}} — negotiable differences. Worth a dedicated conversation.
results.divergentDesc: {{dims}} — notable gap. Prioritize discussing these before major commitments.
results.disclaimer: ⚠️ This assessment is based on self-reported scenarios and cannot replace lived experience. High alignment ≠ definitely compatible; divergence ≠ incompatible.

high.label: Highly Compatible
high.desc: Your decision tendencies align closely across most key dimensions. Great start, but keep re-aligning as people change.

moderate.label: Mostly Compatible
moderate.desc: Most dimensions align, a few differ. Differences are fine if you can discuss them honestly and find workable agreements.

low.label: Needs Deeper Talk
low.desc: Several dimensions differ clearly. Before big decisions, discuss the larger gaps and understand the roots.

critical.label: Significant Gaps
critical.desc: You differ a lot on key decision dimensions. It does not mean you cannot be together, but it requires intentional negotiation. Consider counseling.

high.label: Running Smoothly
high.desc: Your current patterns align across most dimensions. This is a good stability signal.

moderate.label: Needs Tuning
moderate.desc: Overall works well, with a few friction points worth a focused conversation.

low.label: Needs Active Work
low.desc: Several dimensions show notable friction. If left unaddressed, it will accumulate. Start with one or two priorities.

critical.label: Deep Conversation Needed
critical.desc: Major pattern gaps across important dimensions. Not the end, but a clear signal to face it. Consider couples counseling.

couple.label: Dating / Couples
couple.sublabel: Based on tendencies and expectations, explore your alignment

married.label: Married / Cohabiting
married.sublabel: Based on real patterns, identify friction and growth areas

shareCard.footer: Generated by Resonance · Partner Compatibility Tool
